pull.title: refactor: disable default std::hash for CTransactionRef
pull.body:
While working on #33922 I initially forgot to add `CTransactionRefComp` to the `std::unordered_map<CTransactionRef` defined there. This PR turns that into a compiler error.

This change triggers a false positive IWYU error, and an inconsistent one at that: our CI wants `<variant>`, while a manual build on Ubuntu (version 0.26 with clang version 22.1.1) wants `<string_view>`.

Various workarounds were discussed in:
- https://github.com/include-what-you-use/include-what-you-use/issues/2007
- https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/35073
- https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/33922#discussion_r3100806462

The approach chosen here is:
- narrowly scoped to this file
- does add unneeded includes
- does not ignore the false positive `<variant>` / `<string_view>` requirement (as that could cause future bugs)
- works independent of the exact IWYU build
- is skipped for libc++, because that doesn't have the false positive and the workaround triggers an ambiguous specialization.

The main downside is that it's slightly verbose, though mostly from the comment.

events.body: The following sections might be updated with supplementary metadata relevant to reviewers and maintainers. ### Reviews See [the guideline](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#code-review) for information on the review process. | Type | Reviewers | | ---- | --------- | | Approach ACK | [hebasto](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/35101#pullrequestreview-4178536950) | If your review is incorrectly listed, please copy-paste <!--meta-tag:bot-skip--> into the comment that the bot should ignore.
Since this isn't urgent, I think I'll wait for https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/35101#discussion_r3109296011 to land in a release.
I took the IWYU patch to see if it actually does the job (I still plan to wait for it to be backported to the `clang_22` branch).

The IWYU patch [works](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/actions/runs/24879701419/job/72844896011?pr=35101). I also implemented @hebasto's suggestion: https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/35101#discussion_r3105584831

Apparently the patch won't be backported: https://github.com/include-what-you-use/include-what-you-use/pull/2013#issuecomment-4313034887

So I'll just keep it here.

verification.payload:
tree 5528876c0bb36e195a7bb5b689765374ad09530e
parent 1fa34f90a2ff19f62c3e2984baf66b400c66356c
author Sjors Provoost <sjors@sprovoost.nl> 1777018649 +0200
committer Sjors Provoost <sjors@sprovoost.nl> 1777136896 +0200

ci: backport iwyu PR 2013 std::hash mapping

Cherry-pick include-what-you-use commit 52f85e1f4d99 onto the clang_22
branch tracked by ci/test/00_setup_env_native_iwyu.sh, fixing the false
positive where std::hash was mapped to <string_view>/<variant> instead of
its real provider (<functional>, <memory>, etc).

Cherry-pick include-what-you-use commit 52f85e1f4d99 onto the clang_22
branch tracked by ci/test/00_setup_env_native_iwyu.sh, fixing the false
positive where std::hash was mapped to <string_view>/<variant> instead of
its real provider (<functional>, <memory>, etc).

verification.payload:
tree 84756c859b92c6ea1eb61982714506fd8300067f
parent 4e65e4dcd4ef93269c54e2f841a15683b1f6be56
author Sjors Provoost <sjors@sprovoost.nl> 1777019594 +0200
committer Sjors Provoost <sjors@sprovoost.nl> 1777136911 +0200

refactor: disable default std::hash for CTransactionRef

The default std::hash for shared_ptr compares by pointer.

CTransactionRefSaltedHash or a custom hasher should be used instead.

Co-authored-by: Hennadii Stepanov <32963518+hebasto@users.noreply.github.com>

events.message:
refactor: disable default std::hash for CTransactionRef

The default std::hash for shared_ptr compares by pointer.

CTransactionRefSaltedHash or a custom hasher should be used instead.

Co-authored-by: Hennadii Stepanov <32963518+hebasto@users.noreply.github.com>

events.body:
> Apparently the patch won't be backported: [include-what-you-use/include-what-you-use#2013 (comment)](https://github.com/include-what-you-use/include-what-you-use/pull/2013#issuecomment-4313034887)
> 
> So I'll just keep it here.

FWIW, the IWYU maintainer [wrote](https://github.com/include-what-you-use/include-what-you-use/pull/2013#issuecomment-4313034887):
> But if actually backported, it should be done along with the two preceding commits.

Previous two commits, I don't think we need them here:
- https://github.com/include-what-you-use/include-what-you-use/commit/28d70e6a776c735fec8fe5ad630657533000591b: about false positive includes of `<print>`, which afaik we haven't encountered, and is unrelated to this change anyway. 
- https://github.com/include-what-you-use/include-what-you-use/commit/d1e42bf8bebae7269ae83e999b49f11144afeeda: related to `<iosfwd>`, so also seems unrelated to this PR

comments.diff_hunk:
@@ -403,4 +403,27 @@ struct CMutableTransaction
 typedef std::shared_ptr<const CTransaction> CTransactionRef;
 template <typename Tx> static inline CTransactionRef MakeTransactionRef(Tx&& txIn) { return std::make_shared<const CTransaction>(std::forward<Tx>(txIn)); }
 
+namespace std {
+/**
+ * Redeclaration of the primary template outside libc++. Workaround for:
+ * https://github.com/include-what-you-use/include-what-you-use/issues/2007#issuecomment-4259615468
+ *
+ * Skip this on libc++, where std::hash is declared in an inline ABI
+ * namespace (typically std::__1::hash). Redeclaring it directly in namespace
+ * std would make the specialization ambiguous, and libc++ does not need this
+ * workaround.
+ */
+#ifndef _LIBCPP_VERSION
+template <class T>
+struct hash; // IWYU pragma: keep
+#endif
+/** Disable default std::hash for CTransactionRef to prevent accidentally
+ *  comparing by pointer. Use CTransactionRefSaltedHash or provide a custom
+ *  hasher. */
+template <>
+struct hash<CTransactionRef> {
+    size_t operator()(const CTransactionRef&) const = delete;
+};

@@ -404,6 +404,19 @@ typedef std::shared_ptr<const CTransaction> CTransactionRef;
 template <typename Tx> static inline CTransactionRef MakeTransactionRef(Tx&& txIn) { return std::make_shared<const CTransaction>(std::forward<Tx>(txIn)); }
 
 namespace std {
+/**
+ * Redeclaration of the primary template outside libc++. Workaround for:
+ * https://github.com/include-what-you-use/include-what-you-use/issues/2007#issuecomment-4259615468
+ *
+ * Skip this on libc++, where std::hash is declared in an inline ABI
+ * namespace (typically std::__1::hash). Redeclaring it directly in namespace
+ * std would make the specialization ambiguous, and libc++ does not need this
+ * workaround.
+ */
+#ifndef _LIBCPP_VERSION
+template <class T>
+struct hash; // IWYU pragma: keep
+#endif
comments.body:
276da6236af12bb7aee12f74f8138f2c9bb16176:

This changes follows [this](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/35073#discussion_r3093983379) suggestion, excluding libc++, which is not used in the IWYU CI job.

Looks fine to me.

comments.body: Thanks, marking draft while I study this suggestion.

comments.body: I can confirm that the bug has been [fixed](https://github.com/include-what-you-use/include-what-you-use/commit/52f85e1f4d990f55fc6556d543eb051d79364a16) upstream in https://github.com/include-what-you-use/include-what-you-use/pull/2013.

comments.body: I added `hash() = delete;`, but also kept the earlier variant for clarity.
